result:
Fleshed petition written to `/workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/patron-anima-eval/eval-a/outputs/patron-baseline/engine-refresh-bug.md`.

The thin brief had two complaints (flicker + scroll reset) and one remedy ("more targeted refresh"). I fleshed it out by:

- Naming the reader (operator babysitting a running guild) and the actual decision (should I intervene on this engine?), which grounds why scroll preservation matters more than flicker.
- Tightening scope to just the Spider engine-detail panel — explicitly excluding other Oculus pages, transport changes (no websockets), and visual redesign.
- Specifying the behavior I want: diffed DOM updates, `scrollTop` preserved across polls in every scrollable subregion, tail-follow when pinned to bottom, poll cadence unchanged.
- Calling out fabrications (that it's a polling React-ish client, that there's more than one scrollable region, that tail-follow is desirable) as assumptions.
- Deferring three questions for the planner: worst-case engine type, whether a shared live-data hook exists that should be fixed centrally, and whether this fix should pave the way for push transport later.
